data.title: SK Hynix Plans U.S. ADR Offering Amid Expansion Goals
data.description:
South Korean chip manufacturer SK Hynix has announced its intention to issue American Depositary Receipts (ADR) by submitting confidential filing materials to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC). According to RTHK, the company has also filed documents with South Korean regulatory authorities, indicating plans to complete the listing within this year. However, details regarding the issuance size, structure, and timeline have yet to be finalized.

Previous reports from South Korean media suggested that SK Hynix is considering raising between 10 trillion to 15 trillion Korean won, approximately $6.7 billion to $10 billion, through the U.S. listing. The company aims to expand its production capacity for advanced memory chips as part of its growth strategy.

data.title: Meta Faces Challenges in EU Rollout of Ray-Ban Smart Glasses
data.description: Meta's introduction of its new Ray-Ban smart glasses in the European Union is encountering obstacles due to battery and artificial intelligence regulations, as well as supply limitations. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting these challenges as Meta attempts to expand its presence in the wearable technology market. The regulatory environment in the EU is proving to be a significant hurdle for the tech giant, impacting the availability and distribution of the smart glasses. Despite these setbacks, Meta remains committed to addressing these issues to ensure a successful launch in the region.

data.title: Middle East Conflict Resolution Progress Leads to Oil Price Decline
data.description: Oil prices have decreased amid indications of progress in resolving the ongoing Middle East conflict. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting that recent diplomatic efforts have contributed to easing tensions in the region, which is a significant factor affecting global oil markets. Analysts suggest that the potential for a peaceful resolution could stabilize oil supply routes and reduce the risk premium associated with geopolitical uncertainties. The market response reflects optimism that a resolution could lead to more predictable oil production and distribution, impacting global energy prices. As negotiations continue, stakeholders are closely monitoring developments that could influence future oil market dynamics.

data.title: RBNZ Economist Calls for Structural Reforms to Boost Productivity
data.description: RBNZ Chief Economist Paul Conway has called on the government to implement structural reforms to enhance productivity. Bloomberg posted on X, emphasizing that monetary policy alone is insufficient to address the ongoing cost-of-living crisis. Conway's remarks highlight the need for comprehensive strategies beyond monetary interventions to tackle economic challenges effectively.

data.title: Spot gold is at $4,550 per ounce, up 1.69% on the day.
data.description: According to Gate data, spot gold has risen above $4,550 per ounce, up 1.69% on the day.

data.title: Literm, with 97 million monthly downloads, was attacked by the supply chain; a simple installation could steal all sensitive credentials, including SSH keys.
data.description: Andrej Karpathy posted on the X platform that litellm suffered a PyPI supply chain attack. A simple `pip install litellm` command could steal SSH keys, AWS/GCP/Azure credentials, Kubernetes configurations, Git credentials, environment variables, encrypted wallets, SSL private keys, CI/CD keys, and database passwords. litellm has 97 million monthly downloads, and the risk could spread to all projects that depend on litellm, such as dspy. The maliciously injected version was released less than an hour ago, and was discovered when a flaw in the attack code caused Callum McMahon's machine to run out of memory and crash. Karpathy stated that supply chain attacks are one of the most threatening problems in modern software, as each dependency installation can introduce tampered packages deep into the dependency tree. He is therefore increasingly inclined to reduce dependencies and use LLM to directly implement simple functions.

data.title: WTI crude oil fell below $88 per barrel, down 1.01% on the day.
data.description: According to Gate data, WTI crude oil fell below $88 per barrel, down 1.01% on the day.

data.title: LY Corp to Become Largest Shareholder of Kakao Games with $216 Million Investment
data.description: LY Corp is poised to become the largest shareholder of Kakao Games following an investment of approximately 300 billion won ($216 million). According to NS3.AI, this move will see Kakao, currently the top shareholder, become the second-largest stakeholder. Kakao Games announced on March 25 that the investment and shareholding restructuring aim to bolster its global market expansion and future growth strategies.

data.title: AI TRENDS | SK Hynix Plans U.S. Listing Amid Rising AI Memory Demand
data.description: SK Hynix has announced its intention to pursue a listing in the United States within this year. Bloomberg posted on X that the move is part of the company's strategy to secure funds necessary to meet the growing demand for memory driven by advancements in artificial intelligence. The South Korean semiconductor manufacturer aims to bolster its financial resources to stay competitive in the rapidly evolving AI sector. As AI technologies continue to expand, the need for advanced memory solutions has become increasingly critical, prompting SK Hynix to seek additional capital through a U.S. listing. The company is positioning itself to capitalize on the burgeoning AI market, which requires substantial investment in memory technology to support its development and application.

data.title: An account with over $3.3 million in profits purchased $150,000 worth of NBA regular season game footage showing the Hornets defeating the Kings.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that in the Polymarket "NBA Regular Season Hornets vs. Kings" prediction event, an account with over $3.3 million in profits (address: 0x14964aefa2cd7caff7878b3820a690a03c5aa429) bet $158,000 on the Hornets defeating the Kings, with an opening price of 93¢. The NBA regular season Hornets vs. Kings game started at 7:00 AM Beijing time this morning and is currently in halftime. The current score is 72-47, with the Hornets leading by 25 points. The price for a Hornets victory is currently quoted at 99.6¢. Odaily Seer continuously monitors the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Singapore to Uphold Trade Principles Despite Potential Short-Term Costs
data.description: Singapore will maintain its commitment to rules-based trade principles, even if it results in short-term economic costs, according to a report by the Business Times. Bloomberg posted on X that Deputy Prime Minister Gan Kim Yong emphasized the importance of adhering to these principles to ensure long-term stability and growth. He highlighted that while there may be immediate challenges, the adherence to a rules-based system is crucial for sustainable development. Gan's remarks underscore Singapore's dedication to maintaining a fair and predictable trading environment, which is vital for the country's economic resilience.

data.title: Bittensor Subnet Tokens Surge as TAO Rises 90% in a Month
data.description: Bittensor subnet tokens have reached a combined valuation of approximately $1.5 billion, driven by a significant increase in the value of TAO, which rose about 90% over the past month. According to NS3.AI, CoinGecko's Bittensor Subnets category experienced a 30% increase over the past 24 hours, reaching $1.47 billion, with trading volume surpassing $118 million. The surge is attributed to growing interest in Bittensor's decentralized AI model, with τemplar tokens rising 444% over the last 30 days.

data.title: Bank of Japan Member Cautions on Rising Interest Rates Impact
data.description: A member of the Bank of Japan (BOJ) expressed concerns about the potential downward pressure on consumption due to rising interest rates, according to Jin10. However, the member noted that the overall impact on the financial system is likely to be limited. This statement was part of the minutes from the BOJ's January meeting, highlighting the central bank's ongoing assessment of economic conditions and monetary policy. The BOJ continues to monitor the effects of interest rate changes on various sectors of the economy, aiming to maintain stability while addressing potential risks.

data.title: Japan's Central Bank Considers Further Rate Hikes Amid Low Real Interest Rates
data.description: Japan's central bank members have unanimously agreed that it is appropriate to continue raising interest rates, given the significantly low real interest rates. According to Jin10, the January meeting minutes reveal that if the outlook for economic activity and prices is realized, further rate hikes will be considered. Most members believe that policy adjustments should be decided at each meeting based on the prevailing circumstances.

data.title: Fuel Shortages Hit Australian Service Stations Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Fuel shortages have been reported at numerous service stations across Australia due to disruptions in global supplies caused by the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of the geopolitical tensions on the availability of fuel in the region. The situation has raised concerns about the stability of fuel supplies and potential price increases as the conflict continues to affect international markets. Authorities are monitoring the situation closely to address the challenges faced by service stations and consumers.

data.title: U.S. Reportedly Seeks One-Month Ceasefire to Advance Negotiations
data.description:
The United States is reportedly pursuing a one-month ceasefire to facilitate negotiations. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this development as part of broader efforts to address ongoing conflicts. Meanwhile, Iran has begun imposing transit fees on certain merchant vessels passing through strategic straits, signaling a shift in its maritime policies.

In another significant move, China Investment Corporation is reportedly considering resuming its asset allocation in the United States. This potential shift indicates a renewed interest in U.S. markets by one of China's largest sovereign wealth funds.

These developments are part of a broader focus on global market dynamics, with implications for international trade and economic relations.

data.title: Global Grain Production at Risk Due to Fertilizer Supply Disruptions, Goldman Sachs Reports
data.description:
Goldman Sachs has reported that disruptions in nitrogen fertilizer supplies through the Strait of Hormuz could lead to a decline in global grain production and alter planting decisions, potentially driving up grain prices. According to Jin10, the report highlights that a shortage of fertilizers might delay or reduce nitrogen fertilizer application, resulting in lower grain yields and prompting farmers to switch to crops like soybeans, which require less fertilizer.

Data from the U.S. Fertilizer Institute indicates that in some years, up to 50% of urea fertilizer used by American farmers is imported. With supply levels still about 25% below normal, spring planting could face challenges. Goldman Sachs noted that since the onset of the conflict, nitrogen fertilizer prices, which account for approximately 20% of grain production costs, have risen by 40%. Supply disruptions could tighten fertilizer availability in other regions and increase production costs.

While U.S. farmers remain relatively unaffected due to early procurement before the planting season, disruptions in Europe, Australia, and the Southern Hemisphere could boost demand for U.S. grain exports and elevate U.S. grain prices.

data.title: U.S. President Donald Trump Supports Hungarian Prime Minister Orban's Re-election
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed his support for Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban's bid for re-election. According to Jin10, Trump praised Orban's leadership and policies, highlighting the strong bilateral relations between the United States and Hungary. Trump emphasized the importance of maintaining stability and continuity in Hungary's political landscape, suggesting that Orban's continued leadership would benefit both nations. The endorsement comes as Hungary prepares for its upcoming elections, with Orban seeking another term in office. Trump's backing is seen as a significant gesture, reflecting the close ties between the two leaders and their shared political views.

data.title: Airgas to Reduce Helium Shipments After Qatar Production Halt
data.description: Airgas, a leading U.S. distributor of packaged gases, announced it will reduce helium shipments. Bloomberg posted on X that this decision follows Qatar's suspension of operations at a significant liquefied natural gas facility due to an Iranian strike. The halt in production is expected to impact the global supply of helium, a critical component in various industries, including healthcare and technology. The situation underscores the vulnerability of supply chains to geopolitical tensions, particularly in regions with concentrated production facilities. Airgas is assessing the situation and exploring alternative sources to mitigate the impact on its customers.

data.title: Climate Scientist Resigns from NASA Amid Administrative Changes
data.description: Prominent climate scientist Kate Marvel has resigned from NASA, citing significant changes under U.S. President Donald Trump's administration as a key factor in her decision. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting Marvel's concerns about the direction of climate science and policy during this period. Her departure underscores the challenges faced by scientists in navigating political shifts that impact research and environmental strategies. Marvel's resignation reflects broader concerns within the scientific community regarding the administration's approach to climate issues.
